investmentBaseTypes.label: Global Equity
investmentBaseTypes.helpText: Represents broadly diversified equity investments like global index funds or ETFs. For reference, the average growth rate of the S&P500 over the last 30 years has been around 10%.

investmentBaseTypes.label: Global Bonds
investmentBaseTypes.helpText: Represents diversified bond investments (government or corporate). Bonds typically offer lower returns than equities but with lower volatility.

_calibrationDocs.zeroSumConstraint: Σ(π_i × meanModifier_i) = 0 for each assetClass, where π is the stationary distribution computed from transitionMatrix (not manually stated probabilities).
_calibrationDocs.stationaryDistribution: Stationary distribution π is computed from transitionMatrix via power iteration (π_{n+1} = π_n × T) until convergence. This is the reference weighting for the zero-sum check.
_calibrationDocs.transitionRowSum: Each row in transitionMatrix must sum to 1.0.
_calibrationDocs.volatilityMultipliers: Must be positive (> 0). No zero-sum constraint applies.
_calibrationDocs.addingRegimes: Add entry to regimes[], add row and column to transitionMatrix, re-verify all constraints.
_calibrationDocs.configurability: The engine is fully data-driven: regime keys are arbitrary strings, any number of regimes is supported, and all parameters are tunable via config with no code changes required.
